title: [phase:plan] [family:approval-tokens] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Review the new prototype and propose the minimum scoped approval-token model to replace coarse --approve. Return concise bullets: token fields, validation rules, TTL, and how to bind token to action+...
summary_text: - Lock scope first: do not publish just 2 files if they depend on untracked readiness files; current minimal safe publish set is `/.github/workflows/notebooklm-runtime-smoke.yml`, `/tests/test-notebooklm-runtime-smoke.sh`, `/scripts/check-notebooklm-runtime-readiness.sh`, `/tests/test-check-notebooklm-runtime-readiness.sh`. - Run only local smoke/readiness checks before publishing: `bash tests/test-notebooklm-runtime-smoke.sh` and `bash tests/test-check-notebooklm-runtime-readiness.sh`. - Stage path-limited only (dirty tree safe): `git add -- <4 files above>`; avoid `git add .`. - Commit/push only that set: one commit with a narrow message (NotebookLM runtime smoke + readiness gate), then `git push`. - Optional live smoke dispatch (no comments, no CI triage loop): `gh workflow run notebooklm-runtime-smoke.yml -f issue_number=<NotebookLM-hinted-issue> -f post_result_comment=false -f notebooklm_runtime_environment=notebooklm-runtime -f subscription_runner_label=fugue-subscription -f notebooklm_sensitivity=internal -f notebooklm_nlm_bin=nlm`. - If you insist on publishing only 2 files, skip live dispatch; otherwise workflow will likely fail due to missing untracked dependencies. - Keep two active lanes during dispatch (execution + safety monitor); single-lane fallback is a workflow violation.
